summary: I'm Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Program Development & Operations professional who thrives on coordinating strategic initiatives, developing processes grounded in data, and aligning stakeholders for sustainable organizational impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can improve operational efficiency and team alignment—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Empower HR leadership team to present executive-ready deliverables on $362K in costs, driving {coord|policy redesigns} and {coord|process improvements}
Consult with executive leadership to redesign accountability structures and clarify {cross|cross-functional} roles in support of a 2-year {prog|strategic plan}
Manage end-to-end lifecycles for 20+ {prog|cross-functional initiatives}, tracking {coord|milestones} and progress to drive seamless execution
Develop {coord|process documentation} and {coord|operational resources} for 70+ staff, streamlining workflows and reducing onboarding time by 75%
Coordinate {cross|cross-functional} needs assessments with 25+ global {cross|stakeholders} to inform {prog|strategic planning} and learning program design
Coordinate {coord|communications} and training materials for 600+ participants, supporting program delivery and driving 75% enrollment rate
Led manager effectiveness {prog|strategic initiative}, translating data into executive-ready reports to inform {cross|leadership} decisions and strategy
Analyzed 2,000+ employee data points, communicating {data|insights} to {cross|C-suite stakeholders} to guide decisions on organizational priorities
Supported leadership in advancing {prog|strategic initiatives} through consultations and training spanning DEI and performance management
Coordinated 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focused on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D to improve employee experience
Led {coord|change management} coordination for an M&A project, aligning {cross|cross-functional stakeholders} and documenting decisions and outcomes
Streamlined {coord|onboarding processes} across 3 global locations via {coord|process improvement} and gap analysis, clarifying cross-team responsibilities
Initiated a DEI pilot with an Inclusive Leadership workshop for 12 senior leaders, later scaled to 23 global locations
Delivered {prog|strategic HR solutions} in talent management and succession planning, ensuring {cross|organizational alignment} and confidentiality
Managed cross-functional HR operations spanning recruitment, compensation analysis, and performance management globally
Coordinated end-to-end international talent acquisition, including job descriptions, postings, and global compensation benchmarking
Drove a 566% increase in applications in 2 months through personalized global outreach and stakeholder engagement
Planned and executed recruiting events and C-suite panels at top 3 universities, engaging 230+ students and generating 80+ applicants

bullets: Relevant Coursework: Organizational Psychology, Research Methods, Group Dynamics & Leadership, Consumer Psychology

education.degree: BA in Psychology and Education Studies (GPA: 4.00)

projects: Consulting Projects: Enhanced Elanco Animal Health employer branding & talent attraction strategy via survey design/execution
Master's Capstone: Examined non-monetary factors shaping employer attractiveness for YouTube via mixed-methods

jd.meta: WGU — Program Development Operations Manager · Salt Lake City, UT · $88,300–$132,400

sections.title: Role Overview
sections.content: Provides {coord|strategic and operational support} to Program Development leadership. Coordinates {prog|strategic initiatives}, strengthens {coord|communications}, and helps establish {coord|processes} to keep teams {cross|aligned on priorities}. Central resource for {coord|change management} and ensuring key decisions are communicated clearly.

items.text: {coord|Coordinate projects and initiatives} across teams; track {coord|milestones, deliverables}, and progress
Coordinate {coord|leadership communications}; prepare materials for initiatives and priorities
Support {prog|strategic initiative} planning, tracking, and execution
Bachelor’s degree in business, org leadership, PM, or related ✅
Experience supporting projects, strategic initiatives, operations ✅

sections.title: ✅ H1B Status
sections.content: WGU = accredited university → CAP-EXEMPT. No lottery risk. Green light.
